WARNING: These test points are for A2485 (820-02382) ONLY. EVT schematic -- component designators may differ from production. Same X2728 MLB platform as A2442 but 16" form factor with dual fans and larger battery.
description: Decision trees for 20V DCPS on A2485. Normal boot current is HIGHER than A2681 Air (2-4A vs 1.5-2.5A) due to Pro/Max SoC power. Max variant draws more than Pro. 16" may draw slightly more than 14" due to larger display + dual fans.

scenarios.meaning: Board draws nothing. CD3217 or charger circuit completely non-functional.

steps.measure: Probe PPBUS_AON decoupling near battery connector (page 27)
steps.if_wrong: Charger IC not converting. Check CHGR_AUX_OK, I2C to charger.

steps.if_present: PMU not loading -> check U7700/U8100 connections

scenarios.meaning: Board partially active but well below normal 2-4A boot range. A shorted rail is clamping a regulator.

steps.action: Run full chain: PPBUS -> 3V8 -> 5V -> MPMU -> Viper -> Monaco
steps.next: Identify which stage has low/missing output

steps.measure: Diode mode on PP3V8_AON_MPMU, PP3V8_AON_SPMU, PP3V8_AON_WLBT, PP3V8_ATC01_LDO, PP3V8_ATC2_LDO branches (page 33)
Diode mode on Viper outputs (page 46) and Monaco outputs (UA100 page 50, UA200 page 51)
steps.if_wrong: VR output shorted. Thermal cam to find hot SoC pin or decoupling cap.

steps.measure: Check PMU test points (page 129) for each MPMU output
steps.if_any_missing: Thermal cam that rail -> replace hot component or U8100

steps.measure: Check SMC_I2C0 (charger, page 56), SMC_I2C1 (UPC, page 56) pull-up voltage (PP3V3_AON)

scenarios.count_note: Below normal boot range for Pro/Max
scenarios.meaning: Board drawing boot-level current for an Air, but insufficient for Pro/Max. SoC partially powered but Viper or Monaco may not be starting.

steps.if_missing: Viper not starting. Check U9300, VIPER_ALERT_L. Check power stages (pages 48-49). This is the most likely cause of stalled boot on Pro/Max at this current range.

steps.measure: Probe Monaco C0 output (UA100 area, page 50) and C1 output (UA200 area, page 51)
steps.if_missing: Monaco not starting. MONACO_ALERT_L. Check page 53 for CLVR throttle. Max variant: more phases -> more failure points.

steps.measure: Check NAND power: PP0V9_NAND0, PP0V9_NAND1, PP1V2_NAND0, PP1V2_NAND1 (Ocarina pages 101/106)
Backlight on? Image on external HDMI?
steps.if_no_display: Display power sequencer (page 110) or Luxe backlight (page 111) fault

steps.measure: Both FAN0 (left, JE500) and FAN1 (right, JE510) spinning?
steps.if_one_dead: Fan controller (UE500 or UE510 on page 66) fault. Check FAN_PWR_EN, SMC_FAN_x_TACH.
steps.note: 16" has dual fans. Fan failure may cause thermal throttle but not prevent boot.

scenarios.meaning: NORMAL for M1 Pro (2-3.5A) and M1 Max (2.5-4.5A). If customer reports a fault, it's peripheral not power.

steps.measure: Display, keyboard, trackpad, speakers, USB-C ports, HDMI, SD card, fans

sub_checks.check: Luxe backlight (page 111), display power sequencer (page 110), eDP connector (page 109)
UF400 (ATC0 left rear, page 67), UF500 (ATC1 left front, page 68), or UG400 (ATC2 right, page 77). Test each.
Fan controllers UE500/UE510 (page 66), FAN_PWR_EN, SMC_FAN_0_TACH, SMC_FAN_1_TACH. A2485 has DUAL fans.
Check specific fan connector JE500 (left) or JE510 (right) on page 66. Check UE500 or UE510.
Codec (page 115), PP3V8_CODEC_VP, PP1V8_CODEC_VA. Speaker amps on AP_I2C1 and AP_I2C3 (page 55).
Charger IC (U5200, page 28), CHGR_AUX_OK, battery connector (page 27). 99.6 Wh battery.

scenarios.meaning: Excessive even for Max. Major short or GPU under extreme load.

steps.if_functional: May be charging deeply discharged 99.6 Wh battery. Monitor -- should settle to 2-4A.

steps.if_found: Identify component. Common: U5700 (regulating into 3V8 short), Viper/Monaco (shorted SoC domain), SoC (dead -- BER).

steps.measure: Diode mode: PPBUS_AON -> PP3V8_AON (pages 33-34) -> PP5V_S2 (page 54) -> Viper (page 46) -> Monaco (pages 50-52)
steps.process: Find shorted rail. Thermal cam that specific area.
